segments.text: Okay, hello to the class from data request to data awareness and success.
I will be hosting this presentation together with Jan Bonen from Autodesk, he's a technical
specialist. I myself, I am Kevin Amaschalk, I'm acting as a bid manager for the company SBE.
The agenda for today, first of all, we will give you some insights on who are we and who are we
representing to give you some background. Then we will start the class with what is OTR.
This was the start of our journey, so it's quite important that you understand what the basic
components are in that fact. Then we will hop over to our approach that we have done to reach
the result and that's our tool that covers how to handle data and how to fill data and those things,
so we will give a short demo at the end of that one. Then Jan will hop in, so he will cover
what's next and the steps you will undertake in the upcoming months and year.
So first of all, who are we? Well, I am Kevin Amaschalk, as said before, I am born and raised in
Lokeren. I have a mechanical background and after that when I left high school, I started working
at SBE. In 2007, I really started as a cat designer. I was modeling steel constructions,
especially in Bokot and also in Tekla. In 2013, management wanted to install a mechanical
department within the company and I took the role to lead that implementation in SBE.
Then, 2017, I think somewhere in that year, management decided to follow the BIMTrain that was
passing by and now as a scientist in the company to see what the advantages of BIMT could be for
our organization but also for our projects and since then I was really busy with implementing BIMT
in projects within the company and following some trainings and since 2021, I am acting as a
BIMT manager in the company. Then over to Jan. Thank you, Kevin. Nice to meet you all, Jan Bonner,
technical specialist at Autodesk now for more and no just for two years and starting my career as
cat designer in the past where I did some stuff in AutoCATS as well in Revit. Later on, I did some BIMT
consultancy via Geonius and there was also the first interaction with SBE in 2017. Where after I go
to a TIG group in engineering firm and now focus on the site of Autodesk, top of different
infrastructure, cloud solutions and broader portfolio over to Kevin again.
So yeah, which company am I representing? I'm representing SBE. We're an independent
engineering design consultancy company. We are based in Cineclass in Lokeren, that's our headquarters,
but we also have offices in Naman, Rotterdam and Valencia to serve the clients in Belgium,
the Netherlands and in the northern piece of France. We have 35 years of experience at the moment
and I have around 300 colleagues and the goal of SBE really is to gather with the client to build
two marvellous worlds in a sustainable way. And as is last slide about SBE in which areas are
reactive so that you can place the tool that you're building and also the OOTL in which areas are
they popping up. So first of all civil engineering and infrastructure, so road design,
together with everything, sewage, also the tunnels and bridges that belong to civil engineering and
infrastructure. A second area that we're really focusing in is Pratt, Port, River and Hydraulic
Engineering, so especially logs, gables, dams, together with all mechanical equipment and steel
constructions that belong with it. And we also have a team that is covering urbanism and design
that is involved in the early design phases of a project. So now you know who we are and the
company that we're representing and the areas that we have a focus in. So the next step is what
was the question we received from Agenskapiegen Everkir and that was to deliver OOTL compliance.
So first of all OOTL, it's an object type library it is made by Agenskapiegen Everkir and actually
it is an inventory of all the assets that they own and that they need to maintain through the
complete lifecycle. And so main components that are really important are especially three. So
first of all the physical elements that you can see in a real world, like floor slab, a wall,
a lightning post and those things, those are called underdailing and further in the presentation
also mentioned as object types or classes. Then of course to be capable of doing asset management
in a good way, you need information, a set of properties that belong to a class and those properties
are called attributes or attributant by Agenskapiegen Everkir and then one key component is also
that they have relations, relases. The most important relation for SBE is that belong to
relationship or relation and actually that relation is grouping object types or classes to an
installation and I will give an example later on. Important to mention as well is that OOTL is
constantly improving and also that and by improving I mean it's doubled in size if you look at it
in 2022 and if you look at it at 2025. And the mandatory data in the native models is really limited.
You need to have the typeory or the class in your native models and also an asset ID identificator
and that is actually a GUIT, a global unique identifier.
An example of an installation it's a it's a bridge so the main installation the top level is a bridge.
It consists out of sub-installations it can be a bridge deck and a button and pillars and you can
divide them even in more sub-installations until you reach the bottom level the light gray boxes
actually that are the underdelen or the type duties and within the company we're modeling on
the level of the typeuries and we need to find a way to support the belongs to relation to group
those parts typeuries into installations and sub-installations. Also a visible example here of an
abutment can be found so we're really modeling the foundation slabs the walls the wing walls
consoles and then we need to be capable of grouping those elements into an installation or a sub-installation
in this case an abutment. So now at least we have the basics of the OTL and now we can go further
on how did we approach this question and yeah it all started in 2022 in the beginning we were
just following the the setup of OTL what was it containing how it was it how was it set up is
it following some standards or not and at some points projects were coming in that we needed to
deliver OTL compliance so from that moment on we were actively making some proof of concepts
and based on the proof of concepts we selected one and in 2023 we really started the development of
the tool we have today and that's the tool we're still maintaining and upgrading every year.
So the first step was actually the inventory it was analyzing data because yeah that is key
and a data is key in the complete OTL so we were comparing the data that we had in our models
with the data that was asked for by OTL and the first thing we saw was okay data was present in our
models at the moment as well as the fact that we already had naming conventions or we agreed upon
naming regarding the name of the properties or the attributes and the values of it so that were
already two key things key takeaways ways and then the third thing was that we also had a
property in our models that was the standard specifications standard bestec and that specification had
a relation a mapping with the OTL so also that was already an added value had or an advantage we had
but then if we were comparing the data that was asked in OTL with the data that we had in our models
we saw immediately that the data that we had was two general we had length we had area we had
volume and sometimes await but it was really generic over all the items we had in our models but
at that moment we realized that we needed to make a shift in how we were yeah
talking about data within the company we needed to make this shift to relevant object driven data
and after a while we decided to place relevant design data at the core of our enterprise
and that that data should be key in delivering all of our products models drawings and so
but also that that relevant design data is the key to start and design already with the digital
twins so actually with relevant design data we can already make a good start and design phases to
in the end deliver a digital twin so that was really the shift that we made from general data to
relevant design data to support a digital twin early on in the process
an example of that one is a foundation pile so in the beginning we had maybe some length and we had
a diameter and probably await but if you look at those families now we also have top level of
installation bottom of level bottom level of installation we have angles so we have way more
data in our models and also we took it one step further we were checking the complete
product that we needed to deliver in the end and also in our drawings we were showing
text fields but now those text fields are connected to properties within our model so everything
that we show and that we derive from the model starts from a property that is in our model so
the tables the tags everything is derived from data that is filled in our models
then once we did that we were checking the capabilities of the software packages we had in house
we have civil 3d we have rabbit inventor we have navisworks we have tecla and we were checking
how they were dealing with data first of all are they supporting a GUIT yes or no can they
support different property sets can they map attributes can you work with default values drop
downless all those those kind of things that is what we have checked for the different packages we
had in house but then we realized if we were developing on those software packages we needed to
do the implementation multiple times so we already had a common data environment present in our
company Autodesk forma or Autodesk construction cloud and then the next thing was let's check the
capabilities of Autodesk construction cloud together with Autodesk platform services of course
and we saw there that we could read model data immediately and that it was supporting different
data types as well as drop downless and default values and you can already guess this is the part
we took to go third in development and why did we do the development on our own because we had
already experienced in previous tooling we have an in house IT department that is really good in
databases and programming and we got support of management so we had time and budget
and the first step is yeah you receive an SQLite database that contains the definition of the
OOTL but it's just flat rough data and we need to visualize the data and how it's structured in models
in a way and that's why we built an installation parser to show the installations that we have
until you see the underdeal level or the type duties so that's the first check so really take flat data
and show it in another way so that we can understand it in a good way so that is the installation
parser that's the first thing we built then we had clear inputs we had structured design data
we have the typeory and asset idea that are present in our native models and our models were
located on Autodesk format then what did we want to do we want to be capable of
configurating which properties to read to set up a database a second step is we want to be
capable of loading mapping files that help us filling the other data fields automatically
and per project we needed to be capable of loading an OOTL definition so that are the setup
that we wanted to do and step by step we came until the process that is shown underneath so in the
center our SBE tool is there and our tool is first of all reading the models using APS that are
stored on Autodesk format based on the typeory our tool will read the SQLite database and it will
know which properties that to create the link between the model and the database is the asset
ID identifier the GUIT and in the next step by the use of the mapping files and the typeory
our tool knows where to find data in the models and where to fill it in the database as an end
deliverable to the client we can export the database to an xalasix or a csv file that we need to
deliver on a certain tool that against HOPVG in Everkir has made for us to show this a bit more
in depth so we receive an OOTL definition flat data second step is we have models located on
Autodesk format that contain relevant design data as well as the asset ID and the typeory based
on those things a database is created and in the end the database will be filled automatically

so you have a viewer we can select objects on the left side you still see the model data that is

so first of all we need structured data and that is what we can show easily using Autodesk

second thing we can do is check if the relevant data is present in our models and that's also what
